cwe: CWE-611 - Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
CWE-827 - Improper Control of Document Type Definition
CWE-776 - Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs

threat.title: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection in Java, .NET, PHP, and Python Applications
threat.description: XML External Entity (XXE) injection attacks exploit vulnerable XML processors that parse untrusted XML input containing malicious external entity declarations. When XML parsers are configured with default settings that allow external entity resolution, attackers can leverage Document Type Definitions (DTDs) to access local files, perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks against internal services, cause Denial of Service through billion laughs attacks, or execute remote code. XXE vulnerabilities are particularly dangerous in enterprise applications that process SOAP web services, XML-based APIs, file uploads (SVG, DOCX, XLSX), and configuration files. Modern frameworks like Java's SAXParser, .NET's XmlReader, PHP's libxml, and Python's lxml are vulnerable when not properly configured, making XXE one of the most critical web application vulnerabilities affecting data confidentiality and system availability.

technology.primary: Java XML Parsers / .NET XmlReader / PHP libxml / Python lxml
versions_affected: Java SAXParser (all versions with default config)
Java DocumentBuilderFactory (all versions with default config)
Java XMLInputFactory (all versions with default config)
Python xml.etree.ElementTree (all versions with external entity resolution enabled)

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies XML input point: SOAP API endpoint, XML file upload, RSS feed parser, or configuration file import
2. Attacker crafts malicious XML payload containing DOCTYPE declaration with external entity definition pointing to sensitive local file (e.g., /etc/passwd, C:\Windows\win.ini, application config files)
3. Attacker submits malicious XML: <?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">]><user><name>&xxe;</name></user>
4. Vulnerable XML parser processes DOCTYPE declaration, resolves external entity by reading local file system
5. Parser replaces &xxe; entity reference with file contents
6. Application returns processed XML in response or error message, disclosing sensitive file contents to attacker
7. Attacker escalates: performs SSRF to access cloud metadata (http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/), internal services, or launches billion laughs DoS attack using recursive entity expansion
8. Attacker gains: database credentials, AWS keys, source code, internal network access, or causes complete application denial of service
prerequisites: Application accepts and parses XML input from untrusted sources
XML parser configured with external entity resolution enabled (default in most parsers)
DOCTYPE declarations are not disabled or sanitized
Application has file system read access or network connectivity
Error messages or responses leak parsed XML content
No input validation or XML schema validation enforced

cve_references.description: Thoughtworks XStream XXE vulnerability - remote attackers can read arbitrary files via crafted XML with DOCTYPE declaration
Apache Axis XXE vulnerability in SOAP message processing - allows reading local files and SSRF attacks
XStream XXE vulnerability - remote code execution via crafted XML input processing
XStream XXE Server-Side Request Forgery vulnerability - enables SSRF attacks against internal networks
Apache Axis2 XXE vulnerability in SOAP with Attachments processing

real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unpatched XML parser with external entity resolution enabled in public-facing web service

XXE via SVG image upload leads to full file system read access and internal network SSRF
XXE in DOCX/XLSX upload functionality exposes database credentials and AWS keys

security_research.title: XXE (Xml eXternal Entity) Injection
security_research.key_finding: XXE attacks can be used to extract data, execute remote requests, scan internal systems, and perform denial-of-service attacks. All XML parsers should disable DTD processing and external entity resolution by default.

security_research.title: XML Out-Of-Band Data Retrieval
security_research.key_finding: XXE attacks can exfiltrate data using out-of-band techniques when direct responses are not available, using FTP, HTTP, and DNS protocols for data extraction.

security_research.title: Exploiting XXE Vulnerabilities in File Upload Functionality
security_research.key_finding: Office documents (DOCX, XLSX), SVG images, and PDF files commonly contain XML that can be exploited for XXE attacks through file upload features, enabling sophisticated attacks against file processing systems.

security_research.title: Automated Discovery of XXE Vulnerabilities
security_research.key_finding: XXE vulnerabilities remain widespread in modern web applications with 20-30% of tested enterprise applications vulnerable to XXE attacks due to insecure default parser configurations.

mitigations.title: Disable DTDs and External Entity Resolution in XML Parsers
mitigations.description: Configure XML parsers to completely disable Document Type Definitions (DTDs) and external entity resolution. This is the most effective defense against XXE attacks and should be implemented in all XML processing code. Disable DOCTYPE declarations, ENTITY declarations, and all external resource resolution features.

additional_code_examples.code:
from defusedxml import ElementTree as DefusedET
from defusedxml.ElementTree import ParseError
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml_securely(xml_input):
    """
    Securely parse XML using defusedxml library.
    This prevents XXE, billion laughs, quadratic blowup attacks.
    """
    try:
        # Use defusedxml instead of standard xml.etree
        tree = DefusedET.fromstring(xml_input)
        return tree
    except ParseError as e:
        raise SecurityException(f"XML parsing blocked for security: {str(e)}")
    except DefusedET.DTDForbidden as e:
        raise SecurityException(f"DTD detected and blocked: {str(e)}")
    except DefusedET.EntitiesForbidden as e:
        raise SecurityException(f"External entities blocked: {str(e)}")

def parse_xml_stdlib_hardened(xml_input):
    """
    If you must use stdlib xml.etree, configure it securely.
    Note: defusedxml is strongly preferred.
    """
    # Monkey-patch to disable entity resolution
    from xml.etree.ElementTree import XMLParser
    
    class SecureXMLParser(XMLParser):
        def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
            super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
            # Disable entity resolution
            self.parser.EntityDeclHandler = None
            self.parser.UnparsedEntityDeclHandler = None
    
    parser = SecureXMLParser()
    try:
        tree = ET.fromstring(xml_input, parser=parser)
        return tree
    except ET.ParseError as e:
        raise SecurityException(f"XML parsing error: {str(e)}")

class SecurityException(Exception):
    pass

# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    safe_xml = "<user><name>John</name></user>"
    
    try:
        tree = parse_xml_securely(safe_xml)
        print(f"Parsed securely: {tree.tag}")
    except SecurityException as e:
        print(f"Security error: {e}")

<?php

class SecureXMLParser {
    
    public static function parseXMLSecurely($xmlInput) {
        // Disable external entity loading globally
        $previousValue = libxml_disable_entity_loader(true);
        
        // Suppress warnings and errors to prevent information leakage
        libxml_use_internal_errors(true);
        
        // Clear any previous errors
        libxml_clear_errors();
        
        try {
            // Create DOMDocument with secure settings
            $dom = new DOMDocument();
            
            // Disable entity substitution
            $dom->substituteEntities = false;
            
            // Disable resolving externals
            $dom->resolveExternals = false;
            
            // Load XML with LIBXML_NOENT disabled (prevents entity expansion)
            $result = $dom->loadXML(
                $xmlInput,
                LIBXML_DTDLOAD |    // Load DOCTYPE (but don't resolve externals)
                LIBXML_DTDATTR |    // Default DTD attributes
                LIBXML_NONET |      // Disable network access
                LIBXML_PARSEHUGE    // Allow large documents
            );
            
            if (!$result) {
                $errors = libxml_get_errors();
                libxml_clear_errors();
                throw new Exception(
                    "XML parsing failed: " . 
                    ($errors ? $errors[0]->message : "Unknown error")
                );
            }
            
            // Validate no DOCTYPE present
            if ($dom->doctype !== null) {
                throw new Exception("DOCTYPE declarations are not allowed");
            }
            
            return $dom;
            
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            throw new Exception("XML parsing blocked for security: " . $e->getMessage());
        } finally {
            // Restore previous setting
            libxml_disable_entity_loader($previousValue);
            libxml_use_internal_errors(false);
        }
    }
    
    public static function parseXMLSecurelySimpleXML($xmlInput) {
        libxml_disable_entity_loader(true);
        libxml_use_internal_errors(true);
        
        try {
            $xml = simplexml_load_string(
                $xmlInput,
                'SimpleXMLElement',
                LIBXML_NONET | LIBXML_DTDLOAD | LIBXML_DTDATTR
            );
            
            if ($xml === false) {
                throw new Exception("SimpleXML parsing failed");
            }
            
            return $xml;
        } finally {
            libxml_use_internal_errors(false);
        }
    }
}

// Example usage
try {
    $safeXML = '<user><name>John</name></user>';
    $dom = SecureXMLParser::parseXMLSecurely($safeXML);
    echo "Parsed securely: " . $dom->documentElement->nodeName . "\n";
} catch (Exception $e) {
    echo "Error: " . $e->getMessage() . "\n";
}

?>

nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Test with XXE payload containing <!DOCTYPE> declaration - should be rejected
Verify external entity references are blocked
Test with billion laughs DoS payload - should fail safely
Confirm error messages don't leak sensitive information
Validate schema validation is performed when available

mitigations.title: Use Safe XML Parsing Libraries and Configurations
mitigations.description: Prefer XML parsing libraries that are secure by default or use wrapper libraries specifically designed to prevent XXE attacks. Use defusedxml for Python, modern .NET XmlReader with secure settings, and always validate library versions for known vulnerabilities.
validation_steps: Verify XML parser library versions are up-to-date
Confirm secure configurations are applied in all parsing code
Test with various XXE payloads to ensure blocking
Review all XML processing code paths for secure parser usage

mitigations.title: Implement Input Validation and XML Schema Validation
mitigations.description: Validate all XML input against strict XML schemas (XSD) before processing. Implement allowlists for permitted XML elements and attributes. Reject any XML containing DOCTYPE declarations or unexpected structures. Sanitize error messages to prevent information disclosure.
validation_steps: Test with XML missing required elements - should fail validation
Test with XXE payload - should be rejected during sanitization
Verify schema validation catches malformed XML
Confirm error messages don't expose internal details

mitigations.title: Apply XML Security Best Practices and Defense in Depth
mitigations.description: Implement multiple layers of defense including: network segmentation to prevent SSRF, file system access restrictions, Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block XXE payloads, security monitoring to detect XXE attempts, and regular security testing. Use Content Security Policy (CSP) headers and implement least privilege principles for application file system access.

code_example.code:
# ModSecurity XXE Prevention Rules
# Include in modsecurity.conf or custom rules file

# Block DOCTYPE declarations
SecRule REQUEST_BODY "<!DOCTYPE" \
    "id:1001,\
    phase:2,\
    deny,\
    status:403,\
    msg:'XXE Attack Detected: DOCTYPE declaration blocked',\
    logdata:'Matched Data: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
    severity:'CRITICAL',\
    tag:'application-security',\
    tag:'attack-xxe'"

# Block ENTITY declarations
SecRule REQUEST_BODY "<!ENTITY" \
    "id:1002,\
    phase:2,\
    deny,\
    status:403,\
    msg:'XXE Attack Detected: ENTITY declaration blocked',\
    logdata:'Matched Data: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
    severity:'CRITICAL',\
    tag:'application-security',\
    tag:'attack-xxe'"

# Block SYSTEM keyword in XML
SecRule REQUEST_BODY "SYSTEM\\s*[\"']" \
    "id:1003,\
    phase:2,\
    deny,\
    status:403,\
    msg:'XXE Attack Detected: SYSTEM entity reference blocked',\
    logdata:'Matched Data: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
    severity:'CRITICAL',\
    tag:'application-security',\
    tag:'attack-xxe'"

# Block PUBLIC keyword in XML
SecRule REQUEST_BODY "PUBLIC\\s*[\"']" \
    "id:1004,\
    phase:2,\
    deny,\
    status:403,\
    msg:'XXE Attack Detected: PUBLIC entity reference blocked',\
    logdata:'Matched Data: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
    severity:'CRITICAL',\
    tag:'application-security',\
    tag:'attack-xxe'"

# Block file:// protocol in XML
SecRule REQUEST_BODY "file://" \
    "id:1005,\
    phase:2,\
    deny,\
    status:403,\
    msg:'XXE Attack Detected: file:// protocol blocked',\
    logdata:'Matched Data: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
    severity:'CRITICAL',\
    tag:'application-security',\
    tag:'attack-xxe'"

# Block common XXE payloads
SecRule REQUEST_BODY "(?:<!\\[CDATA\\[.*?file:///|<!\\[CDATA\\[.*?/etc/passwd)" \
    "id:1006,\
    phase:2,\
    deny,\
    status:403,\
    msg:'XXE Attack Detected: Suspicious payload pattern',\
    logdata:'Matched Data: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
    severity:'CRITICAL',\
    tag:'application-security',\
    tag:'attack-xxe'"

# Enable request body inspection for XML
SecRule REQUEST_HEADERS:Content-Type "(?:application|text)/xml" \
    "id:1007,\
    phase:1,\
    pass,\
    ctl:requestBodyProcessor=XML,\
    msg:'XML content detected, enabling body inspection'"

iso27001_controls: A.16.1.4 - Assessment and decision on security events
validation_steps: Test WAF rules block XXE payloads with 403 responses
Verify network segmentation prevents SSRF to internal services
Confirm security monitoring alerts on XXE attempts
Test file system restrictions prevent arbitrary file access
Review security logs for XXE detection accuracy

indicators: XML requests containing <!DOCTYPE declarations in request bodies
XML with <!ENTITY declarations or SYSTEM/PUBLIC keywords
File path strings in XML (file://, /etc/passwd, C:\Windows\win.ini)
Error messages exposing file contents or internal paths
Unusual outbound DNS queries from web servers
Large XML payloads indicating billion laughs DoS attempts
Multiple failed XML parsing attempts from same source IP
HTTP requests to cloud metadata endpoints from application servers
XML processing followed by immediate file system access patterns

log_queries.query:
index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=application_logs
| where (match(request_body, "(?i)<!DOCTYPE") OR 
         match(request_body, "(?i)<!ENTITY") OR 
         match(request_body, "(?i)SYSTEM\\s*[\"']") OR
         match(request_body, "(?i)file://") OR
         match(request_body, "/etc/passwd"))
| eval xxe_indicators = case(
    match(request_body, "(?i)<!DOCTYPE"), "DOCTYPE",
    match(request_body, "(?i)<!ENTITY"), "ENTITY",
    match(request_body, "(?i)SYSTEM"), "SYSTEM",
    match(request_body, "file://"), "FILE_PROTOCOL",
    match(request_body, "/etc/passwd"), "PASSWD_ACCESS"
  )
| stats count by src_ip, xxe_indicators, uri_path, status_code
| where count > 0
| sort -count

let XXEPatterns = dynamic(['<!DOCTYPE', '<!ENTITY', 'SYSTEM', 'PUBLIC', 'file://', '/etc/passwd', '169.254.169.254']);
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| union AppServiceConsoleLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where CsMethod in ('POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH')
| where CsUriStem contains "xml" or CsContentType contains "xml"
| extend RequestBody = tostring(parse_json(RequestBody))
| extend XXEIndicator = extract_all(@"(<!DOCTYPE|<!ENTITY|SYSTEM|PUBLIC|file://|/etc/passwd|169\.254\.169\.254)", RequestBody)
| where array_length(XXEIndicator) > 0
| project 
    TimeGenerated,
    CIp,
    CsMethod,
    CsUriStem,
    ScStatus,
    XXEIndicator,
    CsUserAgent
| summarize 
    AttemptCount = count(),
    UniqueURIs = dcount(CsUriStem),
    Indicators = make_set(XXEIndicator)
    by CIp, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where AttemptCount > 3
| order by AttemptCount desc

http.request.method: (POST OR PUT OR PATCH) AND 
(http.request.body.content: (*DOCTYPE* OR *ENTITY* OR *SYSTEM* OR *file\:\/\/* OR *\/etc\/passwd* OR *169.254.169.254*)) AND
http.request.mime_type: *xml*

fields @timestamp, @message, requestParameters.body, sourceIPAddress, userAgent
| filter httpMethod = "POST" or httpMethod = "PUT"
| filter requestParameters.body like /<!DOCTYPE|<!ENTITY|SYSTEM|PUBLIC|file:\\/\\/|\\/etc\\/passwd/
| stats count() as xxe_attempts by sourceIPAddress, bin(5m)
| filter xxe_attempts > 2
| sort xxe_attempts desc
